expectations.text: A report.md file was created in the outputs directory
Report correctly identifies this as a Ruby on Rails application
Report contains findings from multiple distinct analysis dimensions
expectations.evidence: 13 sections covering size, god objects, legacy frontend, deps, security, duplication, TODOs, stylesheets, routes, tests, churn, TypeScript, architecture patterns

expectations.text: Report includes a 'what's working well' section
expectations.evidence: Section 13 'Architectural Patterns Assessment' has 'Positive Patterns' subsection with 7 items including queries layer, policies, presenters, Flipper

expectations.text: Findings reference specific file paths and line numbers
expectations.evidence: Extensive: brakeman line refs, scc tables, god object tables with line counts, jscpd duplication stats

expectations.text: Report includes git churn or change frequency data
expectations.evidence: Section 11 'Churn Hotspots' with full table: meeting.rb 214 changes, types.ts 209 changes, meetings_controller.rb 180 changes

expectations.text: Findings are ranked or prioritized with severity levels
expectations.evidence: Priority recommendations table (P0-P4) at the end with clear groupings

expectations.text: Report notes when multiple agents flagged the same area
expectations.evidence: No convergence signals — baseline has no multi-agent architecture

expectations.text: At least 3 findings include concrete improvement directions
expectations.evidence: Every section ends with specific recommendations
